findings.evidence: Roadmap `TauCetiRoadmap/OneParameterSemigroups/README.md:210-212` lists “closure under sums, products, Schur (pointwise) products”; `rg -n "Schur|schur|pointwise|theorem .*mul|fun x => F x \* G x" TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite/Basic.lean` finds no product-closure theorem, only `add` at line 176 and scalar `const_mul` at line 186.
findings.fix: Add a public theorem such as `IsPositiveDefinite.mul`/`pointwise_mul` proving `IsPositiveDefinite (fun x => F x * G x)` from `hF` and `hG`, using the Schur product argument or an appropriate matrix/finite-family lemma.
findings.issue: The closure API exposes only `IsPositiveDefinite.add` and `IsPositiveDefinite.const_mul`, but the named roadmap target asks for closure under products/Schur pointwise products as part of the basic API.

summary: The zero-value and norm-bound API is now exposed, but the roadmap-facing closure surface is still incomplete: pointwise/Schur products are missing.
